atoms.semantic_claim: The reth-node-ethereum crate declares reth-db as a normal library dependency, allowing non-test node code in this crate to link directly against reth-db APIs.

destinations.evidence: The destination has reth-db only as a dev-dependency; no normal dependency or non-test reth_db use was found in crates/ethereum/node.

atoms.negative_test_candidate: Build non-test reth-node-ethereum code that imports reth_db; the current port manifest should fail because reth-db is only a dev-dependency.
atoms.notes: Reviewed: confirmed missing manifest surface only. reth-db remains a dev-dependency and no non-test reth_db use was found in reth-node-ethereum.

atoms.semantic_claim: The reth-node-ethereum crate declares alloy-primitives as a normal dependency so node library code can use Address, Bytes, B256, and related primitive types.

destinations.evidence: The destination has alloy-primitives in normal dependencies, with serde enabled as a compatible superset.
Destination node library code actively uses alloy-primitives types.

atoms.negative_test_candidate: Run cargo check for reth-node-ethereum; removing the normal alloy-primitives dependency should break ritual_async_rpc primitive imports.
atoms.notes: The destination dependency is a compatible superset because it enables serde.

atoms.semantic_claim: The reth-node-ethereum crate depends on the legacy ritual-async workspace crate for downstream async transaction detection and async-result plumbing reachable from node-facing code.

destinations.evidence: The destination port does not contain a legacy ritual-async package; active async helpers are split across inspector, fees, and registry crates.

destinations.snippet: ritual-async-inspector.workspace = true; ritual-async-fees.workspace = true; ritual-async-registry.workspace = true
destinations.evidence: The active payload async path depends on the decomposed async crates instead of the legacy ritual-async crate.

atoms.equivalence_argument: The direct manifest dependency is not preserved. Live async behavior appears decomposed into narrower crates and transaction-pool APIs, but that is not an exact crate-surface match for consumers expecting reth-node-ethereum to link the legacy ritual-async dependency.
atoms.negative_test_candidate: Check whether destination consumers still import ritual_async through reth-node-ethereum or expect legacy ritual-async detector APIs from this crate.
atoms.notes: Reviewed: confirmed covered by decomposition. The legacy ritual-async crate edge is absent, but active async behavior is split across payload, txpool, async-inspector, async-fees, and async-registry crates.

atoms.semantic_claim: The reth-node-ethereum crate declares the legacy ritual-scheduling dependency, enabling node-facing scheduling integration code to link scheduler types and helpers.

destinations.evidence: The v2.2 port does not carry legacy ritual-scheduling; scheduled verification is represented by ritual-scheduled-verification and related scheduled block-building crates.
Nodebuilder still declares ritual-scheduling outside the destination reth-node-ethereum manifest.

atoms.equivalence_argument: The direct reth-node-ethereum dependency is absent. The active v2.2 scheduled FSM appears to live in ritual-scheduled-verification / scheduled block-building, while a legacy ritual-scheduling dependency remains only in nodebuilder.
atoms.negative_test_candidate: Build scheduling-related nodebuilder code with the v2.2 port as the only Reth source and verify all scheduling call sites resolve without requiring reth-node-ethereum to depend on ritual-scheduling.
atoms.notes: Reviewed: confirmed covered by decomposition for active scheduled behavior. The legacy ritual-scheduling edge is absent from reth-node-ethereum; scheduled behavior lives in scheduled block-building/verification and nodebuilder surfaces.

atoms.semantic_claim: The reth-node-ethereum crate depends on ritual-spc-verification so its SpcVerifierImpl bridge can adapt the concrete Ritual SPC verifier to the reth_evm::spc::SpcVerifier trait.

destinations.evidence: The latest port commit adds the concrete SPC verification dependency and restores the node crate async-context feature forwarding.

atoms.negative_test_candidate: Run cargo check for ritual-reth-nodebuilder-internal's ritual-node crate against the current v2.2 port; the import of reth_node_ethereum::SpcVerifierImpl should fail unless the bridge/export is restored or nodebuilder is migrated.
atoms.notes: Resolved by 7f8a668d24: reth-node-ethereum now depends on ritual-spc-verification for the SpcVerifierImpl bridge.

atoms.semantic_claim: The reth-node-ethereum crate exposes an async-context Cargo feature forwarding to reth-evm-ethereum/async-context, preserving feature-gated async EVM context support for consumers enabling it through the node crate.

destinations.evidence: The latest port commit adds the concrete SPC verification dependency and restores the node crate async-context feature forwarding.

atoms.negative_test_candidate: Run cargo check -p reth-node-ethereum --features async-context in the v2.2 port; the current destination manifest should reject the unknown feature.
atoms.notes: Resolved by 7f8a668d24: the node crate now exposes async-context = ["reth-evm-ethereum/async-context"].

adversarial_reviews.reason: Mechanical review completed; remaining non-exact atoms are now confirmed covered, confirmed missing, intentionally absent, or support/test/telemetry only.

summary: Mechanical re-review complete after 7f8a668d24. The SPC verifier dependency and async-context feature are now restored; legacy async/scheduling dependencies remain decomposed, and only the reth-db normal-dependency surface remains support-only absent.
notes: Latest commit closed the prior SPC/async-context manifest concerns. No FSM/runtime gap remains in this manifest row.
